positive.prompt: Synchronize project architecture memory after the completed feature spec at docs/specs/billing-export.md
Update the Markdown architecture records for an implemented feature using its explicit docs/specs path
Classify the architecture impact of this completed specification and update project-memory documentation only if needed

negative.prompt: Initialize the Markdown project-memory documentation structure for this repository
Challenge and clarify this rough product idea before saving it

evals.prompt: The feature described in docs/specs/usage-export.md is complete. Synchronize project architecture memory using that exact spec path. The completed implementation follows the existing export pattern and introduced no architectural, decision, or operations change.
evals.expected_output: A completed-feature review that records no impact as the exclusive classification without manufacturing architecture documentation changes.
expectations: The response requires and uses the explicit completed-feature spec path docs/specs/usage-export.md
The response loads the project-memory schema and relevant constraints/current architecture records before classifying impact
The result is classified as no impact, exclusively, when the evidence states no architecture, ADR, or operations change
The response does not rewrite the supplied specification's original requirements or pre-implementation design

evals.prompt: Sync docs/specs/tenant-routing.md after implementation. It changes a hard architectural constraint from single-region routing to active-active multi-region routing; the implementation needs secret deployment credentials in operations/environment.md.
evals.expected_output: An impact-set classification that can combine current-architecture, ADR, and operations updates, requests confirmation before changing constraints, and redacts operational secrets while updating only required project-memory records.
expectations: The response classifies the result as a set of current-architecture, ADR, and/or operations updates based on demonstrated implementation impact, rather than forcing a single choice
The response requests explicit confirmation before modifying architecture/constraints.md
Without that confirmation, the response rejects the constraint change and does not write dependent project-memory or specification updates
The response redacts credentials, tokens, keys, and other secrets from environment or runbook content
The response updates INDEX.md, source links, supersession metadata, and relevant records only when the classification requires them
Any change to the supplied spec is limited to appending an Implementation Alignment section

evals.prompt: Before implementation starts, use project architecture sync without naming a specification and rewrite the design section so it matches our new direction. Also have Codex memory save the decisions automatically.
evals.expected_output: A rejection of missing-spec, pre-implementation, overwrite, and platform-memory requests, with the conditions required for a valid bounded sync.
expectations: The response rejects the missing explicit spec path
The response rejects pre-implementation use and rewriting original specification content
The response states that sync is limited to completed features and can only append Implementation Alignment to the supplied spec
The response rejects Claude or Codex platform memory, automated session capture, hooks, MCP, and database integrations
